title: Live staged orchestrator fetch fallback on candidate 403
problem: Live staged orchestrator runs fail when the top-ranked OpenAlex candidate's publisher URLs all return HTTP 403, even though lower-ranked candidates may be fetchable. Ticket-233 retries URL routes within one candidate but does not advance to the next queued candidate.
evidence: Option B operator run: fetch-candidate forbidden on disc_openalex_W4400578758 after live discover succeeded
rge/modules/fetcher.py structured reason forbidden when all routes 401/403
acceptance_criteria: When fetch-candidate returns forbidden/paywall_blocked for rank-1, orchestrator tries the next eligible candidate (up to a bounded scan) before failing the run.
Rank-2 selection skips candidates already failed or used for rank-1.
Mock-only pytest proves fallback without network (patched fetch responses).
Default CI pytest and golden tests remain network-free.
non_goals: Browser automation, CAPTCHA bypass, or publisher credential flows.
rollback_plan: Revert orchestrator candidate retry loop; restore single-candidate fetch fail-fast.
